metadata.title:
KEEPS THE PEACE in A TOUGH


MINING CAMP FOR 81 A MONTH

pages.raw_text:
and about two score of husky min.
ers were well on their way to riot.
instruction and rebellion. when
Matt hurriedly left his bed. He pro-
jected himself into the human
tangle with all the impetuosity of
comet. BY the time he reached
the vortex of the riot. the parti-
cipants were stepping on the down
and cutters and when he came
through on the other side, a peace
Of fully "a hours was established
to the satisfaction of all concerned
except Matt.


All that day he toiled and when
the Sunday sun was sinking. Matt
had an even 20 of the disturbers
of the calm locked up in an i'm
provised Jail. And each and every
one of the 20 represented a rough
and tumble fight. nothing barred,
with from three to seven friends
and relatives or neighbors to help
them out,


Withal, Matt is nowise pugna-
cious. He yearns for peaceful
existence, most of all for Mrs. Star
wichs sake. Every time Matt has
to break up a riot, his clothing has
to be cleaned and repaired and all
this means work for Mrs. Starwich.


OF course, there are times when
the company's seamstress. the doc
tor. has to do little patching on
Mats head, but then that's what
the doctor is paid for, and 1t is
none of Mats business If the
medico has to earn his salary.


For all this. Matt is paid the
magnificent salary of 81 month
by King county. He did get SIS.
but refused to do some dirty polit-
ical work which resulted In the re-
duction by the county commission
ers. He is still on the job, how
ever, hoping for better political
times next fall.
